name: Ratatouille
tagline: Provençal stew of eggplant, zucchini, pepper, tomato — slow simmered

hero.alt: Ratatouille with eggplant, zucchini, peppers in tomato
hero.caption: ratatouille — slow Provençal stew

steps.title: Salt and rest eggplant
steps.detail: Cube eggplant. Salt heavily and rest in a colander 20 minutes. Pat dry — pulls bitter juices and prevents oil-bath later.
steps.tip: Skipping this step makes eggplant a sponge.

steps.title: Brown vegetables in batches
steps.detail: Heat olive oil in a heavy pot. Brown eggplant, then zucchini/squash, then peppers — separately so each gets color, not steam.
steps.tip: Crowding = steaming. Take the time, work in batches.

steps.title: Build the base
steps.detail: In the same pot, sweat onion 5 minutes. Add garlic and herbs de Provence. Pour in tomato, simmer 5 minutes.
steps.tip: All the brown bits left from the veg deepen the tomato base.

steps.title: Combine and stew
steps.detail: Return all vegetables to the pot. Stir gently to coat. Simmer covered 25 minutes, then uncovered 10 minutes to thicken. Tear basil over.
steps.tip: Better the next day — flavors meld overnight.

healthFacts: Eggplant cooked in olive oil draws polyphenols out of the skin.
Bell peppers contain more vitamin C per gram than oranges.
